subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Suppose V is finite dimensional and U is subspace of V. Show that U=V if and only if U⁰={0}.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Show that the mapping T: V₃(R)→V₂(R) defined as T(a₁,a₂,a₃)=(3a₁-2a₂+a₃, a₁-3a₂-2a₃) is a linear transformation.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Suppose V is finite dimensional and U is a subspace of V. Then prove that dim(V/U)=dim V - dim U.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Write short note on: i) Cayley-Hamilton theorem ii) Annihilating Polynomials.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let a, b, c be elements of a field F, and let A be the 3×3 matrix over F. Prove that the characteristic polynomial for A is x³-ax²-bx-c and that it is also the minimal polynomial for A.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let A be an n×n triangular matrix over the field F. Prove that characteristic values of A are the diagonal entries of A.
Diagonalize the matrix [[1,0,-1],[1,2,1],[2,2,3]].

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Show that V₂(R) is an inner product space defined by (α,β)=3a₁b₁+2a₂b₂ for all α=(a₁,a₂), β=(b₁,b₂) in V₂(R).

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain Jordan blocks with suitable examples.
Show that two nilpotent linear transformations are similar if and only if they have same invariants.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Find a basis for the space of all skew-symmetric linear forms on Rⁿ.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Find all bilinear forms on the space of n×1 matrices over C which are invariant under O(n,C).
Write short note on following (any two): a) Dual Spaces b) Primary decomposition theorem c) Bilinear forms d) Inner product spaces.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Show that u₁+u₂+u₃ is not a direct sum for given subspaces of F³.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let F be a field of complex numbers and let T: F³→F³ be defined. Verify that T is linear transformation.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Suppose V is finite dimensional and U is subset to V. Show that U={0} if and only if U⁰=V'.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Write short note on: i) Characteristic values and characteristic vectors ii) Algebraic multiplicity and Geometric multiplicity.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let A be a 4×4 real matrix. Show that the characteristic polynomial for A is x²(x-1)² and that it is also the minimal polynomial.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let T be a linear operator on the n-dimensional vector space V and suppose that T has n distinct characteristic values. Prove that T is diagonalizable.
Find the eigen values and eigen vectors of the matrix [[6,-2,2],[-2,3,-1],[2,-1,3]].

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Explain invariant subspace with suitable examples.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Find Jordan canonical form of the matrix [[1,1,2],[1,2,1],[0,1,3]].
If T in A(V) has all its characteristic roots in F, then there exists a basis of V such that matrix representation of T is triangular. Prove it.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Find all Skew-symmetric bilinear forms on R³.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Find all bilinear forms on the space n×1 matrices over R which are invariant under O(n,R).
Write short note on the following (any two): a) Quotient spaces b) Direct sum decomposition c) Self adjoint d) Jordan blocks.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let U₁, U₂ and U₃ be subspaces of a vector space V. Prove that U₁∩(U₂+(U₁∩U₃))=(U₁∩U₂)+(U₁∩U₃).

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let V be a vector space. Show that dim(V)=n≥1 if and only if there exist one-dimensional subspaces U₁...Uₙ such that V=U₁⊕...⊕Uₙ.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Define the annihilator of a subspace W of V. Prove that W⁰ is a subspace of V* and determine its dimension in terms of the dimension of V and W.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let A be a 4×4 real matrix. Show that the characteristic polynomial for A is x²(x-1)² and that it is also the minimal polynomial.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let W be an invariant subspace for linear operator T. Prove that the minimal polynomial for the restriction operator T_w divides the minimal polynomial for T.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: For the matrix A=[[3,1],[2,2]], verify Cayley-Hamilton theorem by finding its characteristic polynomial and substituting the matrix into it.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Consider the matrix A=[[6,1,0],[0,6,1],[0,0,6]]. Find the Jordan canonical form of the matrix A and the corresponding Jordan basis.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let V be an inner product space, and let α and β be vectors in V. Show that α=β if and only if (α|γ)=(β|γ) for every γ in V.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Consider matrix A=[[3,1],[1,3]]. Find the eigenvalues and corresponding eigenvectors of A. Determine the invariant subspaces corresponding to each distinct eigenvalue.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Given basis and linear transformation values, find T(2,-3,4).

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: If V is the space of all polynomials of degree less than or equal to n over a field F, prove that the differentiation operator on V is nilpotent.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Consider a 2×2 matrix G with characteristic polynomial (t+1)²(t-2) and minimal polynomial (t+1)(t-2). Determine the primary decomposition of R² with respect to G.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let A, B in O(n), the orthogonal group of real n×n matrices. Define the bilinear form as B(A,B)=tr(AᵀB). Verify if B preserves the group structure of O(n).

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Write a detail note on skew symmetric bilinear forms.
Write short note on any two: a) Cayley-Hamilton theorem b) Primary decomposition theorem c) Invariant subspaces.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Prove: Let V be a vector space and W be a subspace of V. Then the map η:V→V/W defined by η(x)=x+W, x∈V, is a linear transformation.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Prove that, if V₀ is a subspace of a vector space V, then there exists a subspace V₁ of V such that V=V₀+V₁ and V₀∩V₁={0}.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Find two linear operators T and U on R² such that TU=0 but UT≠0.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Find the characteristic polynomial and the minimal polynomial for the matrix B=[[2,1,0],[0,2,0],[0,0,3]].

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Prove that T is diagonalizable for the given matrix A=[[5,-6,-6],[-1,4,2],[3,-6,-4]]. Find the diagonalizable matrix P such that PAP⁻¹ is diagonal.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Given matrix B=[[5,2],[3,4]], use the Cayley-Hamilton theorem to calculate B²-9B+14 and verify that it results in the zero matrix.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Find the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the symmetric matrix D=[[5,2,-1],[2,3,2],[-1,2,5]].

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: What are the essential properties of inner product spaces? Provide an example of a vector space that satisfies these properties.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Consider matrix A=[[5,4,2],[0,1,0],[0,0,3]]. Find the Jordan canonical form and determine the corresponding Jordan basis.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Show that the set of all continuous functions on [0,1] equipped with inner product ⟨f,g⟩=∫₀¹ f(x)g(x)dx forms an inner product space.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let V be the space of n×n matrices over F. Define T(B)=AB-BA. Prove that if A is a nilpotent matrix, then T is a nilpotent operator.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let C be a 5×5 matrix with characteristic polynomial (t-1)(t-2)²(t-3)². Determine the possible Jordan canonical forms of C.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let T:C³→C³ be a linear transformation with matrix representation Λ=[[2,-i,0],[i,4,-3i],[0,3i,1]]. Determine whether T is Hermitian.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Suppose T:C²→C² is a linear transformation with matrix representation A. Determine whether T is unitary or not.
Write short note on any two: a) Direct sum decompositions b) Unitary and normal linear transformation c) Annihilator of a subspace.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Define subspace of a vector space. Show that the quotient space of finitely generated space is finitely generated.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: State and prove Rank nullity theorem.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Let f be linear functional on R³. Show that there exists a vector a in R³ such that f(r)=r·a.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Define Characteristic polynomials. Show that all eigen values of A*A are real and it is unitary similar to diagonal matrix.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: State and prove Cayley Hamilton theorem.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let T be a linear transformation on a vector space V of dimension n. Suppose that T has distinct eigen values. Then show that T is diagonalizable.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Define adjoint of Linear Transformation. Show that the given transformation T is a linear transformation.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Let W be a subspace of finite dimensional inner product space V and x∈V. Given the inequality condition, show that x∈W⊥.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Show that there is no proper open subspace of an inner product space.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Define Canonical form of linear transformation. Show that every m×n matrix is equivalent to unique matrix in one of canonical form.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Show that all eigen values of hermitian matrix are all real.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Define Jordan canonical form. Reduce the matrix [[1,0,0],[1,1,0],[0,0,3]] into Jordan canonical forms.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Show that a bilinear form on V is a product of linear functional iff it is of rank 1.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: State and prove Gram Schmidt orthogonalization process.
Write short notes on: i) Symmetric bilinear forms ii) Group preserving bilinear forms iii) Inner product space.
